summary: Adopted the two-tier module convention (modules/system + modules/home aggregators) and converted discord-bot.nix to a real options.services.discordBot.enable + mkIf toggle, opted in explicitly only for nandi via hosts/nandi/default.nix + flake.nix extraModules. All 6 plan phases executed with targeted per-phase commits. nix flake check is green, all three cross-builds (nandi/hamsa/garuda) succeed, and closure inspection confirms nextcord present only in nandi's closure. The HM activation package builds. One verification sub-step (hamsa's live `sudo nixos-rebuild switch` + post-switch systemctl/journalctl re-check) could not be completed because sudo is hard-denied by this agent's execution sandbox -- left as a documented outstanding manual step for a human with sudo access; a pre-switch baseline was captured instead.

artifacts.summary: New always-on system module aggregator (11 entries, discord-bot excluded)
New home-manager module aggregator (31 entries, 1:1 match to prior home.nix list)
Converted to options.services.discordBot.enable + config = lib.mkIf cfg.enable
New per-host opt-in: imports discord-bot.nix, sets services.discordBot.enable = true
Wired nandi's extraModules to hosts/nandi/default.nix; collapsed garuda's now-unneeded extraModules
Corrected file table (lines 25-26) to name the real module path and describe per-host opt-in
Corrected extraSpecialArgs divergence paragraph (task 69 fold-in); Option-A recommendation unchanged
Full implementation summary with verification results and the one outstanding manual step

completion_data.completion_summary: Adopted the module convention (always-on aggregators + optional/host-toggled discord-bot.nix) and made the Discord bot a real per-host opt-in: only nandi has it, hamsa/garuda/iso/usb-installer legitimately lose it. All BUILD-level verification (nix flake check, three cross-builds, closure inspection, HM activation build) is green; the hamsa live sudo switch step is documented as an outstanding manual step blocked by this agent's sandbox.

phases_partial: Phase 6: Staging + BUILD + RUNTIME verification (hamsa live switch step blocked by sandbox sudo denial; all other Phase 6 items complete)

partial_progress.details: All phases executed and committed. Phase 6 BUILD verification fully green (nix flake check, nandi/hamsa/garuda cross-builds, closure inspection, HM activation build). Phase 6 RUNTIME verification's hamsa live switch sub-step is blocked: sudo is hard-denied by this agent's execution sandbox (confirmed denied even with dangerouslyDisableSandbox). Pre-switch baseline captured (both services active/running on hamsa, matching documented pre-existing crash-loop). Outstanding manual step documented in the summary for a human with sudo access to complete.

next_steps: A human with sudo access on hamsa should run: sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#hamsa; then systemctl status discord-bot.service opencode-serve.service (expect not-found/inactive) and journalctl -u discord-bot.service -n 20 (expect no new activity). nandi's behavior change is already confirmed via cross-build + closure inspection (not live-reachable from hamsa). After manual confirmation, task 86 can be marked COMPLETED.
